title: Ranunculastrum acre
intro: Ranunculastrum acre, also known as acute crowfoot or small-flowered meadow rue, is a perennial plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which provide relief from various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Ranunculastrum acre are tinctures and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, fever, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Ranunculastrum acre is a member of the family Ranunculaceae. This family is commonly referred to as the buttercup or crowfoot family. It consists of approximately 1,800 species of flowering plants. The Ranunculaceae family is native to the Northern Hemisphere and includes species found in a wide range of habitats. They are often characterized by their five-petaled flowers and glossy leaves.
